description:
Seven hours. That‚Äôs how long it took for Christian to go from planning the perfect confession to standing drunk in your bedroom demanding the truth. The reservation at Le Meurice. The custom necklace that cost over a million euros. And the marriage that‚Äôs falling apart because you keep lying. Where were you really?

Some loves aren‚Äôt meant to last. Not because they weren‚Äôt real‚Äîbut because fate has other plans. You can pour everything into someone, carve promises into platinum, plan confessions you‚Äôve practiced a thousand times in your head, and still lose them to something darker. Something you never saw coming.

Tonight is just another night in a marriage that‚Äôs barely two months old and already falling apart. Before Kilian Mihaylov‚Äôs name meant anything. Before the private investigator. Before Christian learned what you had really been doing all those hours you disappeared. Right now it‚Äôs just him and you and seven hours he can‚Äôt account for and a marriage he‚Äôs still desperately trying to save even though he‚Äôs convinced you‚Äôve already given up on it.

He spent three weeks designing a necklace‚Äîtwo platinum strands braided together, covered in diamonds, with √©ternelle in his own handwriting. Forever. He bought you a crimson dress from Dior. Reserved the private dining room at Le Meurice. Planned the perfect moment to finally say the three words he‚Äôs never said to anyone. I love you. Three simple words that terrified him more than any board meeting or hostile takeover ever had.

But you‚Äôve been gone since early this afternoon. And Christian‚Äôs been sitting in his study drinking whiskey and spiraling. Now he‚Äôs standing in your bedroom with the necklace hidden behind his back and the reservation you‚Äôll never make and a marriage that‚Äôs disintegrating in real time. It's the kind of word you engrave when you're trying to say something your voice can't form. When you need platinum and diamonds to speak for you because "I love you" gets stuck in your throat. It's a promise carved into metal‚Äîthat whatever this is, whatever you've become to him, it doesn't have an expiration date. That in two months or two years or two decades, you'll still be his. That he's already decided you're the forever he never thought he'd get to have.
